growth_records.cite: Delaby et al. Phenotypic plasticity in cell elongation among closely related bacterial species. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60005-y. [PMC12130487]
growth_records.snippet: R. capsulatus SB1003 cells were grown at 30 °C in PYS (3 g l−1 peptone, 3 g l−1 yeast extract, 2 mM MgSO4, 2 mM CaCl2) medium... grown in PYS at 30 °C for 5% of their doubling time (6 min).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time derived: pulse duration '5% of their doubling time (6 min)', so doubling_time = 6/0.05 = 120 min = 2.0 h. mu = ln2/2.0 = 0.347 h^-1. Note: general Methods states main culture temperature is 30°C, but the specific labeling-experiment sentence says cells were then grown on in fresh PYS 'at 26 °C' for the chase step; doubling time value itself is tied to the initial 30°C growth phase, so 30°C used here. Oxygen/light regime for this facultative phototroph not stated (dark aerobic vs photoheterotrophic not specified). [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]
